title: Parse arguments with a clear, conventional grammar
description: Use a real argument parser with consistent flag conventions, subcommands, and a -- separator, not ad-hoc string matching.
body: Use when a CLI takes options or subcommands. Do not hand-roll positional argv indexing or invent inconsistent flag styles, and do not silently ignore unknown flags. Actions: use the platform's arg-parsing library, follow conventions (short -v / long --verbose, --key=value or --key value, -- to end option parsing), define subcommands explicitly, and error on unknown options. Validation signals include `--help` listing every flag, unknown flags producing a usage error (exit 2), and `--` letting filenames starting with - through. Risk boundary: silently ignoring an unrecognized flag can make a destructive command run with unintended defaults — fail loudly on unknown options.
